title: How can I add additional rows to a Monday.doc table?
excerpt: I am trying to create a template Monday doc which includes a table (not a widget).  However, I would like the ability to add additional rows should the need arise.  I can not see anyway of doing this…am I missing somethi…

posts.author: AlanBonfield (Alan Bonfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying to create a template Monday doc which includes a table (not a widget). However, I would like the ability to add additional rows should the need arise. I can not see anyway of doing this…am I missing something simple?

Thanks for any guidance.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @AlanBonfield Welcome to the Community.

Yes this should be simple if you right click or select the table options should appear if you click the three dots it gives you more options to add a new row.

image1053×312 13.9 KB

posts.author: AlanBonfield (Alan Bonfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Clare,

Thank you for your prompt response and apologies for the dim question.

I still don’t get the option when I right click - it just goes to the Apple right click actions rather than Monday ones. However, I have found that a “+” symbol will appear if I hover at the beginning of the row, which allows the extra row to be added.

Thanks again

Regards
Alan

posts.author: Richard396Thompson (Richard396Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!
To add more rows to a table in your Monday.comtemplate, first open your board and click on the table where you want to add rows. Look for the “+” button at the bottom of the table to add a new row. If you don’t see this option, you may need to adjust your board’s settings or layout. Sometimes, the option to add rows can be hidden depending on the board’s configuration.

posts.author: AlanBonfield (Alan Bonfield)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Richard - yep all working now thanks, was just a bit tricky to find the ‘+’ in the beginning.

posts.author: rachelenglish (Rachel English)
posts.cookedHtml:
This doesn’t work, the plus sign, it just adds a block of text below the table. There’s oddly no way to just add a row.

What settings are off in the board or layout? I don’t know how to find what needs to be fixed to add a row.
